title: Race-Lap Pit-Wave Features
summary: F1 strategy writeups emphasize that stops cluster when the pit window opens and rivals become undercut-vulnerable.
rationale: F1 strategy writeups emphasize that stops cluster when the pit window opens and rivals become undercut-vulnerable. Your current best features are mostly row-local; they do not explicitly encode whether this lap is becoming a race-wide pit wave.
implementation_hint: Build past-only race-lap aggregates within each Race-Year-LapNumber: fraction of cars that pitted on the previous 1-2 laps, field percentiles of TyreLife/Cumulative_Degradation/LapTime_Delta, and each driver’s deviation from those percentiles. Add interactions with Position and Compound.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination around the true opening of stop windows, especially when several cars converge on similar stint lengths.
risk: Easy to leak if same-lap target information is used instead of lagged or race-state-only aggregates.
